scope: Current Confluence Cloud page types, editor elements, REST page subtype behavior, and legacy-editor chronology

sources.title: Add elements to a page or live doc
Confluence Cloud REST API v2: Page
Macros that are unavailable or have limited availability in the Cloud editor

findings: Current Confluence content types include published pages, live docs, blog posts, whiteboards, databases, content-tree Smart Link items, and slides; only published pages are inside this skill's mutation contract.
Content-tree Smart Link items are a separate content type from Smart Links embedded in a page body.

The official basic-element list contains action items, code snippets, dates, decisions, dividers, emoji, expands, headings, mentions, panels, quotes, and statuses; the capability matrix accounts for each category and keeps untested option combinations conditional or preserve-only.
The REST v2 page-list endpoint accepts a subtype filter and the same page model can represent live docs with subtype live.
Legacy-editor retirement began in January 2026, while full deprecation occurred on April 1, 2026.
resulting_changes: scan-space now requests subtype=page, validates each returned item before fetching ADF or rendered view data, and requires every continuation to preserve the original space, current status, and page subtype filters.
An unexpected live doc or other unsupported subtype makes an inventory partial instead of entering a published-page batch.
The standalone documentation distinguishes content-tree Smart Link items from embedded page Smart Links and states the April 1, 2026 full-deprecation date.
Unit tests cover the page subtype filter, live-doc rejection, all nine batch CLI command parsers, every CLI dispatch path, and real JPEG ground-truth decoding.
limitations: This dated review is not an exhaustive inventory of Marketplace modules or tenant-specific editor experiments.
Live docs, blog posts, databases, whiteboards, content-tree Smart Link items, folders, and slides require dedicated workflows before mutation support can be claimed.
